summary: c=2 MoE/GEMV path sweep after full-attention value-scan work. Forcing selected_c1_batch MoE is generated-token green and materially faster than grouped_compact at c=2, but it is diagnostic/non-retained under current retained gates and still below c1 aggregate throughput. Per-row MoE fallbacks are slower and not c-aware retained paths.

retained_claim_blockers: selected_c1_batch is a diagnostic MoE path; current retained c>N artifact validation expects grouped_compact MoE decode for retained throughput rows
selected_c1_batch c=2 aggregate remains below c1 baseline, so aggregate_vs_c1 would still block c2 retained promotion
per-row MoE diagnostics are not native c-aware retained paths and are slower than default grouped_compact

conclusion.reason: The selected_c1_batch MoE diagnostic is the first c2 non-attention path to move aggregate decode materially (best 1.129x default), but it remains diagnostic/non-retained and best 0.941x c1. Next useful work is a retained grouped-compact c2 MoE replacement or schema-backed decision on whether selected_c1_batch can be considered a retained native c-aware path, not more full-attention value-scan tweaks.

commands.sweep: HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 scripts/qwen35_batch_retained_bench.py ... --batch-size 2 --batch-sample-mode batched_lm_head --primitive-correctness-json benchmarks/results/2026-06-02-hipengine-qwen35-native-c2-profiler-preflight/primitive-c2.json --projection-dispatch-artifact benchmarks/results/2026-06-02-hipengine-qwen35-native-c2-projection-dispatch-evidence/summary.json with variants default, --batch-decode-moe-path selected_c1, --batch-decode-full-attn-moe-path per_row_c1, --batch-decode-linear-moe-path per_row_c1
commands.profile_selected_c1: rocprofv3 --kernel-trace --output-format csv -d /tmp/hipengine-e2e-native-c2-selected-c1-moe-profile-iter113 -- env HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 python3 scripts/qwen35_batch_retained_bench.py ... --batch-decode-moe-path selected_c1 --json /tmp/hipengine-e2e-native-c2-selected-c1-moe-profiled-iter113.json
